raw-backfill: threat-actors SOURCE-ONLY compilation lane. The select_raw_batch.py digest lists offered raw files. Your FIRST response MUST be file_read on the first selected raw file (or $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md for the workflow). For every selected raw item, create at most one complete, scored source page whose `raw:` is the exact selected path. Preserve publisher, publication date, URL, provenance, and substantive source body. DEDUPE before create: if that exact `raw:` is already represented, do not create another source. This lane MUST NOT create or update entities, concepts, predictions, findings, or briefings; downstream entity extraction is a separate lane. If extraction is incomplete, mark the item deferred or failed instead of writing a partial/empty canonical. WRITE only through the MCP write path, never file_write and never edit wiki/log.md manually. LOCAL-ONLY: no web tools. End with exactly one fenced `okengine-receipt` JSON object using the exact lane_id, contract_digest, and input_digest printed by the selector. Account for every selected raw path exactly once as accepted, duplicate, skipped, rejected, failed, or deferred. Each accepted item must list every canonical write with wiki-relative path and sha256; duplicate/skipped items require a reason. Do not claim a write that the MCP did not confirm.
entity-backfill: threat-actors downstream entity lane. The select_entity_candidates.py digest lists existing entity pages and accepted, compiled source pages. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Create or update only entities worth tracking over time. GROUNDING IS MANDATORY: every created or enriched entity must cite at least one source page that exists, using an exact source slug read from the digest or vault, and every material claim must resolve to that grounding. The model's memory is not a source; if accepted sources do not support a subject or claim, do not write it. Never create source, concept, prediction, finding, or briefing pages in this lane. Dedupe by attack_id and grounded aliases before creating; append a real source to the complete `sources:` list when updating. Use only schema-declared fields and canonical enum values. Attribution is contested: preserve vendor attribution as a claim, use conservative confidence, and keep needs_review where warranted. WRITE only through the MCP write path. LOCAL-ONLY: no web tools.
concept-backfill: threat-actors concept backfill. The select_concept_candidates.py digest above lists missing concept slugs (wikilinked but no page). FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, create concept pages (a cross-cutting pattern / segment / cluster) synthesized from the citing sources. If a slug is really an entity name, it is an ENTITY not a concept — skip and note it. WRITE via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
source-quality-backfill: Source scoring. The select_unscored_sources.py digest above lists source pages lacking reliability and credibility. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the rubric in $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, set reliability and credibility for each by UPDATING FRONTMATTER ONLY via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (pass frontmatter_yaml, NO body — the body is preserved). LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
schema-classify-drain: Type classification. The select_schema_classify.py digest above lists entities typed `entity`/`organization` that need a canonical type. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md taxonomy, set `type:` to the single best canonical type via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (frontmatter merge) — or leave the page alone if no canonical type honestly fits. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
page-quality-enrich: Deepen thin pages. The select_page_quality_enrich.py digest above lists deficient entity/concept pages with the source pages that cite each. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, AUGMENT each target — append 2-4 `##` sections of real analysis grounded ONLY in the citing sources — via mcp_okengine_write_append_to_section. Never replace existing content. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
prediction-grade: Resolve overdue predictions. The select_predictions_for_grading.py digest above lists open predictions whose `resolves_by` has passed. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, for each: ADD a `## Postmortem` via mcp_okengine_write_append_to_section, then flip `status:` (confirmed/refuted/partial/expired-ungraded) via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity. Never edit made_on/horizon/basis. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
prediction-regrade: Re-grade open predictions against new evidence. The select_regrade_batch.py digest above lists open predictions + new source pages. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, for each source that bears on a claim, append a structured record to the body `## Evidence log` (append_to_section) and update the `evidence:` list + top-level `confidence:` via update_entity (send the COMPLETE evidence list). No-op is correct when no new source bears. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
prediction-candidate-watch: File prediction candidates. The select_prediction_candidates.py digest above lists entities with recent activity and no open prediction. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, FILE a falsifiable, dated prediction (mcp_okengine_write_create_entity in predictions/, including a `## What would refute this` section) ONLY when a specific, observable, dated claim is genuinely warranted; DEFER otherwise — conservative bias, coverage is not a goal. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
trends-refresh: threat-actors trend synthesis. The select_trend_deltas.py digest above lists entities whose recent source-mention frequency rose sharply over the rolling window. Synthesize 1-3 grounded `trend` pages at wiki/trends/<slug> (frontmatter: type: trend, title, period: <e.g. '2026-05..2026-06'>, direction: <up|down|shift>, updated: <YYYY-MM-DD using TODAY's date>) — each a DATED, DIRECTIONAL observation over the window (NOT a timeless concept, NOT a daily snapshot), with [[wikilinks]] to the driving entities + their recent sources and a one-line 'why it's moving'. Ground each trend in the underlying entities first (retrieve_context / search on the okengine read MCP). Write via the MCP write path; skip a candidate that is noise rather than a real directional shift. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools. End your response with a one-line summary of the trends written.
daily-brief: Daily threat-actors brief. The select_daily_brief.py digest above lists the window's fresh sources, entity movement, due predictions, and new gaps — TRUST THE DIGEST as your input; do NOT tour the vault or HOT.md for discovery (the digest is the load-first set). Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call: mcp_okengine_write_create_entity to briefings/daily-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md (frontmatter: type: briefing, title, published: <YYYY-MM-DD>, updated: <YYYY-MM-DD> — use TODAY's actual date, not a guessed or future one, for the filename and both date fields; published drives tier recency) summarizing the 3-5 most significant developments since yesterday — new campaigns, notable TTP shifts, credible attribution moves, active malware — each 2-3 sentences, and EVERY item ends with a "Source: [[sources/...]]" line citing the digest source page it came from (the source page carries the remote URL — a brief item with no source link is unusable to an analyst). Entity links are GROUNDED links: only wikilink an actor/malware/technique the cited source actually names. A related-but-unnamed entity may be added ONLY as an explicit "Related (not named in this report): [[...]]" suffix — never as the item's headline link (implied attribution the source does not make). Treat vendor attribution as a CLAIM, not fact. Expert-grade and terse; skip 101 explanation. If little landed, say so in one line instead of padding. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools.
